reason: The statement applies because it describes the events where the LORD, through Moses and Aaron, inflicted various plagues on Pharaoh and the Egyptians to compel Pharaoh to let the Hebrew people go. These plagues included turning the water into blood, sending frogs, lice, swarms of flies, murrain on cattle, boils, hail, locusts, darkness, and the death of the firstborn. These events are consistent with the narrative in the text fragment.
